plugin_desc: FiberHome (烽火) switch-specific SNMP collection (WRI-CPU/MEMORY/TEMPERATURE/POWER MIBs, IANA PEN 3807). In addition to standard IF-MIB interface metrics, it collects FiberHome private OIDs for device health: CPU utilization (cpuUsage, precision-100), system memory (alloc/total bytes), chassis temperature (temperatureValue) and power-supply state (powerState, where 0=Normal). Fan is exposed only as fan speed (RPM) with no fan-state enum, so fan state is not collected (N/A); fan speed and voltage are additional new-metric candidates left for later enrichment. PSU raw codes are normalized via telegraf processors.enum to 1=healthy / 2=fault. Memory utilization is computed from used/total. Supported brands/models — FiberHome switches.
name: Switch

metrics.name: device_cpu_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports the CPU utilization of the FiberHome device control plane (WRI-CPU-MIB cpuUsage, reported at precision-100 and scaled down by 100), averaged across CPUs and normalized to 0-100. Sustained high CPU usage can indicate control-plane overload, routing instability, or attack traffic, and may lead to slow management response or packet processing delays.

metrics.name: device_memory_total
metrics.description: This metric reports the total size in bytes of the FiberHome system memory pool (WRI-MEMORY-MIB memoryPoolTotalBytes). Together with the used bytes it determines overall memory utilization.

metrics.name: device_memory_used
metrics.description: This metric reports the number of bytes currently allocated in the FiberHome system memory pool (WRI-MEMORY-MIB memoryPoolAllocBytesNum). It helps administrators understand absolute memory consumption and identify when the device is under memory pressure.

metrics.name: device_memory_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports overall memory utilization of the FiberHome device, computed as used / total * 100. High memory usage may indicate memory leaks, oversized routing/MAC/ARP tables, or insufficient capacity, and can cause instability or failed allocations.

metrics.name: device_temperature_celsius
metrics.description: This metric reports the chassis temperature in degrees Celsius of the FiberHome device (WRI-TEMPERATURE-MIB temperatureValue, a dedicated typed temperature column), aggregated as the maximum across sensors. High temperatures can indicate cooling failure or airflow obstruction and risk thermal shutdown.

metrics.name: device_psu_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the power-supply state of the FiberHome device (WRI-POWER-MIB powerState, raw 0=Normal / 1=Insufficient / 2=Overload), normalized via telegraf processors.enum so that 1 = healthy and 2 = fault. Aggregated as the maximum across supplies so any failed PSU surfaces. A value greater than 1 indicates a power-supply problem.

metrics.name: device_fan_speed
metrics.description: This metric reports the fan rotation speed in RPM of the FiberHome device (FAN-MIB fanCtrlSpeed), taken as the maximum across fans. Abnormally low or zero RPM indicates a stalled/failed fan and impending overheating; abnormally high RPM can indicate the device is compensating for high temperature.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCInOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifInOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCOutOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifOutOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.
